cp_1_212: Check the hindwing at vein 3 for a short white-tipped tail: present sends you to the tiny abseus group (2 spp); absent keeps you in the main key. Forewing vein 9 is also very short in abseus.
cp_2_7: The tell is on the underside forewing cell: if the cell spots are outlined in greenish silver you are in the centaurus group (3 spp, large butterflies with a short hindwing cell). No silver edging and a hindwing cell about half the wing or longer means No.
cp_3_6: Yes needs a continuous forewing postdiscal band AND a costal spot in space 10. No identifies A. amantes: its space-4 postdiscal spot is shifted outwards out of line, it has no space-10 costal spot, and a dark patch under the cell reaches the band.
cp_4_5: Separates two centaurus-group species by the hindwing tornus. Yes = A. centaurus (Dull Oak-blue): tornus rounded, tornal lobe vestigial. No = A. araxes: a distinct tornal lobe is present (male also violet-tinged towards the apices).
cp_8_211: Look for a dark bar closing the cell on the underside of both wings. No identifies A. fulla (Spotless Oakblue): a small tailless species whose markings shrink to obscure postdiscal fasciae and a few faint basal hindwing spots, with no forewing end-cell bar.
cp_9_95: Judge the underside hindwing postdiscal spots 7, 6 and 5. Yes = they sit in echelon with centres roughly in line, spot 6 closer to spot 5 than to the end-cell bar. No = spots widely out of line, spot 6 pulled inward towards spot 7 and touching the end-cell bar.
cp_10_23: Check the underside forewing above the central cell spot for a subcostal spot in mid-space 11 and/or at the base of space 10. Yes advances a small set (7 spp); No is the larger branch (note space 10 can rarely show a spot in hellada and aedias).
cp_11_16: Yes = the tailless agesias group: sexes alike, powdery blue above with a broad forewing border widening to mid-costa. No = a tailed hindwing with marked sexual dimorphism. Tail presence and whether the sexes match are the quickest checks.
cp_12_13: Yes identifies A. similis (Druce's Oakblue), the one agesias-group member lacking a forewing postdiscal band; its underside is uniform hair brown with bold rounded spots. No means a normal postdiscal band is present, sending you on to agesias/kinabala.
cp_14_15: Separates A. agesias (Yes) from A. kinabala (Golden-spotted Oakblue, No). agesias: hair brown, rounded spots, a single spot in space 11. kinabala: larger blackish markings, two spots in space 11, and elongate spots touching/overlapping the end-cell bars.
cp_17_18: Yes identifies A. ijanensis (White-banded Oakblue): no spot at the base of forewing space 10 but a spot at the base of hindwing space 6, plus a diagnostic whitish streak running from the dorsum base to the apex. No has the reverse spot arrangement.
cp_19_22: Gauge how far the underside forewing postdiscal band is dislocated at vein 4. Yes = only partially dislocated (anthelus group). No identifies A. anarte: band completely dislocated, spots 4, 5 and 6 in line and oblique; a very large silvery-blue camdeo-group species.
cp_20_21: Separates A. anthelus (Magnificent Oakblue, Yes) from A. achelous (No). anthelus: three spots in forewing space 11 and two spots at the base of hindwing space 8. achelous: no space-11 spots, a single spot at base of space 8, and clearly smaller.

cp_24_45.text: Yes routes exclusively into the camdeo group: a dark area under the forewing cell, bounded outwardly by a pale line running right across space 1b, often with a spot at the extreme base of space 3. Every species reachable from Yes belongs to that group (listed below). No takes the other route — keying instead on the hindwing spot-6 position (spot 6 overlapping, or nearer to, spot 5 than the end-cell bar) — and leads to the amphimuta, hypomuta and aedias groups.

group.label: camdeo group — species reached by answering Yes

cp_25_40: Scan the underside hindwing for a spot mid-space 8 or at the extreme base of space 6. Yes = both absent (advances a set of 8 spp). No = at least one of those spots present.
cp_26_37: Yes = the underside forewing postdiscal band continues into space 9, with marked sexual dimorphism. No = the band stops at vein 7, and the sexes are alike with broad borders widening to about 5 mm at the costa (forewing 18-20 mm).
cp_27_36: Check the underside forewing postdiscal spot in space 4 against those in spaces 5 and 6. Yes = it lies in line or on an even curve with them. No identifies A. hellada (long-banded Oakblue): spot 4 shifted outwards out of line, with a short tail about 2.5 mm.
cp_28_29: Yes identifies A. varro: the forewing postdiscal spot in space 9 continues the line of spots 4, 5 and 6, and the tail is very short (about 1.5 mm); upperside pale metallic silvery blue. No = spot 9 shifted basad out of line and a long tail (4-6 mm).
cp_30_31: Yes identifies A. dispar: underside ground colour mostly white, whitish about the end-cell, with a notably long tail (4-6 mm). No = a uniform grey-brown to hair-brown underside, continuing to the rest of the camdeo subgroup.
cp_32_35: This splits the camdeo subgroup. Yes advances towards A. camdana / A. semperi; No identifies A. opalina (Opal Oakblue), whose hindwing spot 6 is as large as or larger than spot 7, with the space-1b spot not elongate and the end-cell bar not bulged outwards.
cp_33_34: The underside is near-identical, so use size. Yes = A. camdana (small, forewing ~21 mm, upperside pale clear violet blue); No = A. semperi (large, ~28 mm, deep dull bluish purple, female whitish at the end-cell). Forewing length alone usually settles it.
cp_38_39: Both are camdeo-group with the forewing postdiscal band ending at vein 7. Yes = A. johoreana (short tail at vein 2, darker violet blue); No = A. belphoebe, tailless and much paler violet blue with the sexes alike. Check for the tail and the upperside tone.
cp_41_44: Yes keeps to the myrzala subgroup (A. myrzala / myrzalina): crimson-brown, no spot mid-space 8, and the two space-7 spots conjoined at their upper ends. No = A. bazaloides, purple-brown with a spot mid-space 8, a unique extra spot in space 8, and separate space-7 spots.
cp_42_43: Both are crimson-brown myrzala-subgroup; the tail decides. Yes = A. myrzala (Malayan Oakblue), tailed at vein 2, ~21 mm, narrow border. No = A. myrzalina, tailless and small (~16 mm) with broad borders and spot 6 touching or overlapping the end-cell bar.
cp_46_55: On the forewing underside, check whether the postdiscal spot in space 4 sits outside the line of the spots in spaces 5 and 6. Yes narrows to 5 species; No is the larger 21-species branch. Note it can be only slightly shifted in the amphimuta group, muta subgroup.
cp_47_50: Judge where hindwing spot 6 sits between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Yes (nearer spot 5, and no forewing discal patch) heads to A. hypomuta / A. aedias; No is the epimuta group, with spot 6 about midway and a feeble upperside discal patch.
cp_48_49: Tail and the basal spot in space 6 decide. Yes = A. hypomuta (Violet Oakblue), tailless with small rounded spots and a spot at the base of space 6. No = A. aedias (Large Metallic Oakblue), tailed with a long ~6 mm tail and no basal spot in space 6.
cp_51_52: All three are epimuta-group (faint discal patch, spot 6 midway); the tail decides. Yes = A. epimuta (Tailless Disc Oakblue). No, if a filamentous tail is present at vein 2, advances to A. atosia / A. lurida.
cp_53_54: Yes = A. atosia (Tailed Disc Oakblue), with a large oval discal patch reaching the cell apex. No = A. lurida (Lesser Disc Oakblue), smaller patch and, usefully on an underside, darker and better-defined markings; also a little smaller (~19-21 mm).
cp_56_59: Look at hindwing spot 6 relative to spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Yes = agesilaus group (spot 6 roughly midway, tailless) leading to A. agesilaus / A. avatha. No = spot 6 overlapping or closer to spot 5. Field photos can look more displaced than the midway ideal.
cp_57_58: Both are small tailless agesilaus-group; check the forewing basal cell spot. Yes = A. agesilaus (basal cell spot present, ~20 mm, rich violet blue). No = A. avatha (no basal cell spot, smaller ~16 mm, very dark bluish violet).
cp_60_63: Simply whether the hindwing bears a tail at vein 2. Yes leads to the tailed pair A. allata / A. delta; No continues the larger tailless branch (17 species).
cp_61_62: Yes = A. allata (Rosy Oakblue): acute forewing apex, straight termen, and narrow, roughly rectangular hindwing spots 7 and 6. No = A. delta (Deltal Dull Oakblue): rounded forewing, forewing band almost fully dislocated at vein 4, and rounded hindwing spots.
cp_64_71: Look in the hindwing cilia near the tornus for a small white spot in space 1a (plus a very weak tooth at vein 2). Yes narrows to 4 species; No (spot absent or obscure) goes to the 13-species branch. In practice only A. norda and A. moolaiana reliably show this spot.
cp_65_70: Both concern how far the hindwing postdiscal band is broken at vein 2. Yes (only partially dislocated) advances among 3 species; No = A. sceva, where the band is more strongly dislocated at vein 2.
cp_66_69: A male upperside character. Yes (forewing border regular, under 1 mm) heads to A. moolaiana / A. dajagaka. No = A. norda (Langkawi Yellow Oakblue), border widening 2 to 3 mm; also told by a white patch on the hindwing cilia above the tornal lobe and less rounded spots.
cp_67_68: Hard to split; the upperside or genitalia help. Yes = A. moolaiana (Pale Yellow Oakblue), shining blue with the female hindwing border only ~1 mm. No = A. dajagaka (Bornean, dark bluish purple, female border at least 2 mm), often needing genitalia to separate from A. amphimuta.
cp_72_73: Both leads continue to key 73; the No lead describes A. baluensis, a Bornean insect very hard to separate from small A. amphimuta without dissecting the male genitalia (unilobate valva). Its cilia usually lack the white spot in space 1a and are only weakly toothed at vein 2.
cp_73_74: Yes identifies A. baluensis, a Bornean endemic whose underside is essentially inseparable from a small A. amphimuta. Separation relies on the unilobate male valva (dissection) or the dark purple-blue upperside with a fine 0.5 mm border — not judgeable from an underside photo alone.
cp_75_76: Yes identifies A. amphimuta (Broad Yellow Oakblue), a large tailless species. Its underside barely differs from A. major (No side); the split is upperside — amphimuta warm violet-purple with a narrow 0.5-1 mm border, major bluer with a 2-3 mm border. CD-limited from an underside-only photo.
cp_76_77: Yes identifies A. major (Major Yellow Oakblue) — violet-blue above with a border widening from 2 mm at the tornus to 3 mm at the apex. The character is a weak hindwing tooth at vein 2 (inconspicuous when worn); No, with no trace of a tooth, moves to the rounded-hindwing species.
cp_78_82: Splits the tailless amphimuta group by the hindwing postdiscal band. Partially dislocated (Yes) = the spot in space 2 is only slightly stepped from the spot in space 1b; completely dislocated (No) = the band is fully broken into two offset portions at vein 2. Not absolutely constant in A. alica.
cp_79_80: Yes identifies A. zylda — small (FW 16-17 mm) with clear quadrate, banded underside spots and no tooth at vein 2. No leads to A. alica, whose underside markings are almost entirely suppressed (diagnostic). The stated shining-blue vs dull-purple difference is an upperside character.
cp_80_81: Yes identifies A. alica, a Bornean species with underside markings almost entirely absent (only a faint trace of the HW postdiscal band) and a thread-thin border. No leads to A. stubbsi, which shows normal quadrate/banded spots and a much wider 2 mm upperside border.
cp_81_82: Yes identifies A. stubbsi, small (FW ~16.5 mm), separated from the similar A. zylda and A. alica mainly by its much broader 2 mm upperside border (reduced to a thread in those two). Its underside has normal quadrate/banded spots, so the border character needs an upperside view.
cp_83_84: Yes identifies A. avathina (Lunulate Yellow Oakblue). Check the forewing cell base — avathina uniquely lacks the basal spot in the cell. Its hindwing also shows rounded postdiscal spots and darker, better-defined submarginal lunules; No (basal cell spot present, as usual) moves on.
cp_85_86: Yes identifies A. kurzi. Underside cues: forewing postdiscal spot in space 3 elongated towards the base, spot 1b (when present) shifted inward midway between the end-cell bar and spot 2, and a widely dislocated HW band; spots 4 and 5 often appear vertically in line. Hindwing tornus rounded.
cp_87_92: Splits the amphimuta subgroups by the forewing postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3. In echelon, spot 2 stepped outward towards the termen (Yes) = muta subgroup, unilobate valva. In line, though possibly oblique (No) = perimuta subgroup, bilobate valva.
cp_88_89: Yes identifies A. tropaea — very small (FW only 13-14 mm) and hardly shining, with quadrate/banded underside spots and spot 2 strongly stepped out. No (strongly shining and larger) leads on to A. muta and A. moorei.
cp_90_91: Separates A. muta (Mutal Oakblue) from A. moorei (Moore's Oakblue); their undersides are essentially identical. Yes (muta): hindwing a slightly paler, brighter blue than the forewing. No (moorei): both wings the same shade in all lights (valva unilobate). Needs an upperside view.
cp_93_94: Separates A. metamuta from A. perimuta (Yellowdisc Oakblue) on the hindwing underside. Yes (metamuta): brown with green tornal scales. No (perimuta): a central yellowish disc and no green tornal scales. Above, metamuta shows a brilliant greenish-blue hindwing contrasting the forewing.
cp_96_153: A major split on the hindwing postdiscal band at vein 2. Completely dislocated (Yes) = the band is fully broken into two offset portions; only partially dislocated (No) = the spot in space 2 is merely stepped from space 1b. Rather inconstant in A. antimuta, so weigh other characters too.
cp_97_100: A structural split: check for a slender tail (filament) on the hindwing at vein 2. Tailless (Yes) narrows to the two tailless species A. antimuta and A. inornata; tailed (No) opens the large tailed branch.
cp_98_99: Separates A. antimuta (Small Tailless Oakblue) from A. inornata (Inornate Oakblue). Yes (antimuta): green tornal scales on the hindwing. No (inornata): no green tornal scales, hindwing very rounded at the tornus, and larger (FW ~21 vs 16 mm).
cp_101_120: Look at the forewing: a costal spot in mid-space 11 plus the postdiscal spot in space 4 kicked outward, out of line with spots 5 and 6 (Yes), points towards the democritus/alitaeus groups. No spot in space 11, spots 4-5-6 in line, and a band-like HW cell spot (No) = agrata group, ace subgroup.
cp_102_103: Yes identifies A. democritus (White-dot Oakblue): hindwing postdiscal spot in space 6 outwardly concave and a short tail (1.5-2 mm); ground dark chocolate with white striae or hair brown with buff striae. No (spot 6 straight/convex, tail 3.5-4 mm) = alitaeus group.
cp_104_113: Look at the extreme base of forewing space 10, just above the central cell spot. Without a spot there (Yes) versus with one present (No, occasionally absent on one or both sides) splits the alitaeus group into two branches.
cp_105_110: A male upperside border character, not judgeable from an underside photo alone. A thread-thin border (<0.5 mm) keeps you among the faint-marked elopura/arianaga/havilandi trio; a border of at least 1.0 mm that widens towards the costa jumps to the ariana/aida pair.
cp_106_109: 'No' identifies A. havilandi: its underside is pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings and a thread-thin male border. 'Yes' (uniform hair brown, faint markings barely darker than the ground) keeps elopura and arianaga in play.
cp_107_108: Separates A. elopura (Yellow Tailed Oakblue) from A. arianaga. Elopura's markings are barely darker than the ground with HW space 6 partly fuscous, 17-19 mm; arianaga is montane (>1000 m), has a faint pinkish glaze, markings a little darker, space 6 all blue, and is larger at 21-22 mm.
cp_111_112: Yes = A. ariana (Pinkish-washed Oakblue): FW termen slightly convex, apex not produced, underside dark brown with a purplish wash. No = A. aida: FW termen straight, apex slightly produced, paler brown with a slatey or purplish glaze and smaller (16-19 mm).
cp_114_117: A male upperside border character, hard to read from an underside photo. 'Yes' (border at least 1.0 mm) leads to the pseudomuta/alitaeus pair; a thread-thin border ('No') jumps ahead to the sintanga/denta branch.
cp_115_116: Yes = A. pseudomuta (Raffles's Oakblue), No = A. alitaeus. Spot-6 overlap is unreliable between these two; judge by the underside markings instead: pseudomuta is uniform hair brown with faint markings barely darker than the ground, alitaeus pale slatey purple with contrasting purple-brown, macular/annular markings.
cp_118_119: Yes = A. sintanga (pale slatey purple, contrasting reddish-brown markings, dark violet male, tail 3.5-4.0 mm). No = A. denta (hair brown, sometimes a faint pink wash, brilliant blue male, and a short tail ~2.5 mm, about half the length of others in the group). Tail length is the easiest underside cue.
cp_122_123: Yes = A. ace (Tytler's Dull Oakblue): HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave, all markings wide, FW band fully dislocated at vein 4. No = A. azinis: spot 6 straight or convex, band only sinuous or slightly dislocated at vein 4.
cp_125_126: Yes = A. agrata (De Niceville's Oakblue): faint markings, FW postdiscal spot 9 absent or very faint, no costal spot in space 10, HW spot 6 much wider than spot 7. No enters the cleander group, where spot 9 is well-marked, a space-10 costal spot is usually present and all markings are crisply defined.
cp_127_146: Splits the cleander group by tornal lobe and size. Yes = alea subgroup: no conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe, smaller (fwl <21 mm). No = cleander subgroup: a conspicuous projecting tornal lobe and larger (>21 mm).
cp_128_129: Yes = A. aurelia: underside pale slatey purple with conspicuous dark markings outlined by purplish-white striae. No keeps the hair-brown species (markings barely darker than the ground, pale buff striae; evansi carries a slatey glaze) in play.
cp_130_133: A male upperside character. Yes (border widening towards the apex, HW blue stopping short of the termen in space 6) leads to the selta/phaenops pair; No (even border no more than 0.5 mm, HW blue reaching the space-6 termen) advances further into the subgroup.
cp_131_132: Yes = A. selta (Reddish-brown Oakblue): FW termen rounded and HW postdiscal spot 6 widely overlapping the end-cell bar. No = A. phaenops (Sumatran Oakblue): FW termen straight and spot 6 usually not, or only just, overlapping the bar.
cp_134_137: A male upperside colour character; tilt the specimen, as the violet tinge strengthens under electric light. Yes (pure blue, no violet) leads to the sublustris/milleri pair; No (violet-tinged blue) advances to the evansi branch.
cp_135_136: Yes = A. sublustris (Bright Oakblue): bright glistening blue male, border under 0.5 mm, smaller (~18 mm). No = A. milleri: matt, darker indigo, border reduced to a thread, larger (~19.5 mm). On the underside milleri usually lacks the FW costal spot in space 10 that sublustris shows.
cp_138_139: Yes = A. evansi: underside with a slatey/rosy glaze, whiter striae, and FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 upright, in echelon and larger than the spots above them. No (no glaze, at most a faint rosy wash in phanda) continues through the subgroup.
cp_140_143: Yes (HW spot 6 quadrate, its outer edge just meeting or overlapping the inner edge of spot 5; FW costal spot in space 10 usually absent and band barely dislocated at vein 4) advances. No = A. normani: postdiscal band close to the inner submarginal lunules and spot 6 widely overlapping the end-cell bar.
cp_141_142: Judge the gap between the underside postdiscal band and the inner submarginal lunules on both wings. Band hugging the lunules = A. phanda (Golden Oakblue). No routes to A. aenigma, where the band sits well clear (interval in HW spaces 3-5 as wide as the spots) and which is smaller (Fwl ~14.5 mm, Borneo).
cp_143_144: Compare HW postdiscal spot 6. In A. normani it is scarcely narrower at the bottom than the top and widely overlaps the end-cell bar, with the band close to the lunules. No leads to A. aroa, whose spot 6 tapers sharply at the base and barely touches the end-cell bar, its band well separated and wings more elongate.
cp_147_148: Check whether the underside FW postdiscal band steps (dislocates) at vein 4. Unbroken, though possibly sinuous = A. cleander (Large Mergui Oakblue), a large insect with a broad male border. A partial dislocation at vein 4 (and a male border under 1 mm) sends you on to key 149.
cp_149_150: Focus on the FW postdiscal spot in space 9: in A. zambra (Zambra Oakblue) it is as wide as spot 6 and lines up with spots 4-6, and the space-10 costal spot is as broad as the end-cell bar right above it. If spot 9 is narrower and shifted inward (costal spot narrower too), go to key 150.
cp_151_152: Gauge the gap between HW spot 5 and the end-cell bar. In A. athada (Vinous Oakblue) spots 6 and 7 stand upright and the quadrate spot 6 nearly fills that gap, which is no wider than either spot. In A. silhetensis (Sylhet Oakblue) the gap is wider than either spot and spot 6 tapers with a convex/sinuous outer edge well inside spot 5; it also runs larger (Fwl up to 26 mm).
cp_154_202: Look between the basal and central cell spots on the underside forewing (space 11). No spot there (Yes) continues the main branch to key 155. A spot present there, with a short hindwing tail, marks the ganesa group (key 203).
cp_155_191: This turns on hindwing cell length (a male upperside feature, so a dorsal view may be needed) and on the underside FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2-3. A short cell with those spots no wider than those above vein 4 keeps you at key 156; a cell longer than half the wing with spots 2-3 broadened (widest in 3), on a tailed wing, jumps to key 192.
cp_156_179: Check the alignment of the underside FW postdiscal spots in spaces 4-6. A smooth, continuous run (Yes) goes to key 157. If spot 4 is pushed outward (distad), out of line with spots 5 and 6, on a tailed wing, that is the eumolphus group (key 180).
cp_157_158: A. agaba (Purple-glazed Oakblue) shows two tell-tales: HW postdiscal spot 5 touching, or nearly touching, the end-cell bar, and a pale whitish-irrorated subapical patch on both wings over a pale purple-brown ground, usually obvious even in field photos. If spot 5 stands well clear of the end-cell bar, continue to key 159.
cp_159_160: A. vihara (Large Spotted Oakblue) has the underside FW postdiscal band sharply stepped at vein 4, no space-10 costal spot, and a distinctively rounded, isolated HW spot 6 (tailed). If the band is at most weakly dislocated or merely sinuous at vein 4, go to key 161.
cp_161_178: Judge whether the underside FW postdiscal band broadens in its lower half. If spaces 2-3 are no wider than 4-6 (Yes), advance to key 162. A band that widens strongly downward (lower part much broader, space-10 costal spot present, wing tailless) identifies A. epimete.
cp_162_167: Two groups part here. The rama group (Yes, key 163) has a space-10 costal spot, no spot at the base of space 3, and an outwardly sinuous or concave HW spot 6. The agelastus group (No, key 168) lacks the costal spot, often has a space-3 basal spot, and has a convex spot 6 (except A. alaconia).
cp_163_164: A. paralea (a rare montane species) lacks tornal green scales, or shows only vestiges, has a 2.5 mm tail, and a distinctly pale purplish-brown underside. If green tornal scales are present over a plain hair-brown ground, continue to key 165 (buddha/arvina).
cp_165_166: The split is the tail: A. buddha (Buddha Oakblue) carries a short 1 mm tail, whereas A. arvina is tailless, so check the tornal margin at vein 2. Both have a plain hair-brown underside; on the upperside buddha is shining violet blue (border ~1 mm) and arvina darker blue with a thread border.
cp_168_169: A. alaconia (Patchy Oakblue) has the inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2-3 and HW spaces 4-5 conspicuously enlarged and darkened, often bold black wedges with thick pale borders (decisive even in field photos), plus a concave or sinuous spot 6. If all submarginal spots are uniform and spot 6 is convex, go to key 170.
cp_170_171: Within this agelastus-subgroup cluster A. barami (Wood's Oakblue) is the tailed one; the rest (key 172) are tailless, so check for a tail at vein 2. It is also large (Fwl 22-23 mm) with an exceptionally broad male border, up to 8 mm at the costa (an upperside cue).
cp_172_177: A. cardoni has an evenly rounded hindwing with no tornal green scales and small, neat macular markings. If the cilia are toothed (dentate) at vein 2 and green tornal scales are present, continue to key 173 (agelastus and allies).
cp_173_174: Look at the HW postdiscal spots in spaces 2-5. In A. agelastus (Green-edged Tailless Oakblue) they sweep an almost even curve; if the space-3 spot is shifted well inward, out of line with spots 2 and 4, go to key 175 (labuana/wildeyana).
cp_175_176: These two share the inward-shifted HW spot 3 and separate mostly on size and male upperside (a dorsal view helps): A. labuana is larger (Fwl 20-22 mm), dark violet with a narrow border; A. wildeyana is small (Fwl ~16 mm), violet blue with a broader border (2-5 mm).
cp_180_181: Yes clinches A. bazalus (Powdered Oakblue): a dark purplish-brown underside with very dark markings, plus a forewing apex dusted with pale violet scales. No routes to the hair-brown-underside species (key 181+). The pale-violet apical powdering is the easiest tell.
cp_182_187: An underside-friendly split of the eumolphus group by hindwing postdiscal spot 6: outwardly straight goes with the blue/purple males (overdijkinki, acta, tameanga → key 183); outwardly concave goes with the green males (horsfieldi, eumolphus, hellenore → key 188). Upperside colour confirms if visible.
cp_183_184: Yes is A. overdijkinki: a rare, large species with a notably long tail (~5 mm) and very broad rich-violet male borders (2.5 mm at tornus widening to 7 mm at costa). No leads to shorter-tailed (~3 mm), narrow-bordered species (key 184).
cp_185_186: Separates A. acta (Yes) from A. tameanga (No, Borneo). acta has a rounded forewing and faint underside markings; tameanga has a more acute apex, clearly defined well-contrasted markings, and is larger. Marking sharpness is the easiest underside cue.
cp_188_189: Yes is A. horsfieldi (Peacock Oakblue): very broad black male borders reaching the cell on both wings, and broad underside markings. No continues to A. eumolphus/hellenore, which have narrower borders and finer underside markings (key 189).
cp_189_190: Splits A. eumolphus (Green Oakblue, Yes) from A. hellenore (Doherty's Green Oakblue, No). eumolphus has a rounded forewing; hellenore an acute apex with a straight or slightly concave termen and a narrower female border. Undersides are alike, so judge forewing shape.
cp_192_193: Yes is A. corinda (Ultramarine Oakblue): a long filamentous tail (~3.5 mm) and a solid, unbroken dark patch filling the basal half of forewing space 1b. No goes to the aurea group — short stout tail (<2 mm) and a space-1b patch that is not solid.
cp_194_201: Yes (complete markings) advances the aurea group (key 195). No is A. caeca: its underside markings are conspicuously incomplete — no basal or central cell spots on the forewing, several hindwing spots missing, and a narrow postdiscal band. The gaps are the giveaway.
cp_195_198: Ground-colour split within the aurea group. A plain hair-brown underside (Yes) goes to A. aurea/borneensis (key 196); a distinct purple wash over the brown (No) goes to A. trogon/stinga (key 198). Judge the overall underside tint in even light.
cp_196_197: A. aurea (Long-celled Oakblue, Yes) vs A. borneensis (No) — undersides are identical, so use the upperside. aurea: male border 0.75–1.5 mm at tornus, weak basal blue. borneensis: border a thread throughout, brassier green with stronger basal blue, green often reaching the hindwing termen.
cp_199_200: A. trogon (Green-suffused Oakblue, Yes) vs A. stinga (No) — undersides match, so use the male upperside. trogon is brassy green with strong basal blue suffusion, border a thread, green reaching the termen in spaces 4–5. stinga is less brassy, no basal blue, and a wide (2.5–3 mm) forewing border.
cp_203_204: Yes is A. paraganesa (Dusky Bushblue): a quadrate whitish area on the hindwing underside from costa to mid-cell, and no tornal green scales; male upperside pale blue with a mostly fuscous hindwing. No goes to species whose white, if any, is confined to space 7 (key 204).
cp_205_206: Yes is A. ammonides (Little Cerulean Oakblue): no tornal green scales, and the gap between the outer and central spots in hindwing space 7 whitened. No routes to species that DO have tornal green scales (key 207) — check the hindwing tornus for green scaling first.
cp_207_208: Yes is A. ammon (Malayan Oakblue): hindwing spot 6 runs right to the base of the space, and the space-7 spots stay separate with a white-filled gap. No goes to smaller A. ariel/elizabethae — spot 6 normal (not reaching the base) and space-7 spots joined at their upper ends.
cp_209_210: A. ariel (Chocolate Oakblue, Yes) vs A. elizabethae (No): near-identical tiny species (~13 mm), so the male upperside decides — ariel is dull bluish purple, hardly shining; elizabethae is shining deep blue with slightly wider borders.
cp_213_214: A. anella (Yes) vs A. abseus (Aberrant Oakblue, No). anella is larger (20–26 mm) with underside markings not strongly darkened and no forewing costal spot in space 12. abseus is smaller (~17 mm) with much-darkened markings, a space-12 costal spot, and its mid-space-7 hindwing spot whitish-ringed.
cp_121_124: Yes = HW central cell spot band-like, stretching right across the cell → the agrata group's ace subgroup (A. ace / A. azinis). No = cell spot rounded or oval (as usual) → continue to the agrata and cleander groups (key 125 onward).
